_comment: Single source of truth for store identity + per-store submission facts. Consumed by scripts/release.mjs (renderPacket) and any future browser-driver / publishing-API path. Update when a store changes its flow or when the Edge product ID is captured. Facts verified 2026-06-20 — see references/*.md for sources.

chrome.releaseNotesFallback: Chrome has no changelog field. Optionally fold highlights into the detailed description; otherwise the version simply ships.
chrome.reviewEta: usually a few days; occasionally up to ~3 weeks (submission-surge notice as of 2026-04)
declarations: Remote code: select "No, I am not using remote code".
Per-permission justifications (storage, cookies, offscreen, tabs, audio, contextMenus) — canonical text in doc/WEB_STORE_PERMISSIONS.md.
Data-usage disclosures + certifications (not sold; used only for the extension's single purpose).
checklist: Sign in at the Developer Dashboard (2-Step Verification required on the publishing account).
Open the Say, Pi item → Package tab.
Confirm the manifest version incremented (rejected otherwise).
Re-confirm the Privacy practices tab (declarations above).
Submit for review (leave "Publish automatically after approval" checked unless you want to defer go-live).
gotchas: No "what's new" field — users do not see the changelog on Chrome.
Package = the CONTENTS of .output/chrome-mv3 zipped with manifest.json at the root (package-extension.sh already does this).

api.auth: OAuth2 refresh token (or V2 service account); scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chromewebstore
api.note: V2 base https://chromewebstore.googleapis.com — :upload then :publish; cannot create items or change visibility. See references/chrome-web-store.md.

edge.idTodo: capture the Product ID from Partner Center → Extension overview → Extension identity on first run, then record it here as "id"
edge.releaseNotesFallback: Edge has no changelog field; put a short summary of what changed into "Notes for certification".
edge.reviewerNotes: "Notes for certification" — optional but recommended for updates; describe what changed to speed certification.
declarations: Privacy fields may still live under Properties if the May-2026 Privacy-page rollout hasn't reached the account.
checklist: Sign in at Partner Center → Edge program.
Open Say, Pi → Packages → upload dist/saypi.edge.zip.
Publish → on the Submit page, add Notes for certification (what changed).
gotchas: Name / Short description / Description are READ-ONLY on Edge — pulled from the manifest. To change them you re-upload a package.
The listing must not say "Chrome" (certification requirement). Our name/description come from _locales/en/messages.json (appName/appDescription) — grep them if unsure.
No deferred publish: submitting to certification is the irreversible commit point; it auto-publishes on approval (no separate go-live gate).
update_url must be absent from the manifest (it is in our build).

api.auth: API key + Client ID (headers Authorization: ApiKey …, X-ClientID: …); create under Partner Center → Edge → Publish API
api.note: Base https://api.addons.microsoftedge.microsoft.com — upload package → poll → publish → poll. Updates only; cannot create products or edit listing metadata. See references/edge-addons.md.

firefox.releaseNotesLocation: the "Release Notes for this Version" field (shown to users)
firefox.reviewerNotes: "Notes for Reviewers": point to source-code.zip and restate the build (Node ≥22 / npm ≥10, `npm ci && npm run build:firefox`); note the onnxruntime eval() warning is third-party, tree-shaken, never executed.
firefox.reviewEta: signed & published within ~24h; longer (a few days) if selected for manual review
declarations: Source code: Yes (minified/bundled build) → upload source-code.zip.
checklist: Sign in at the AMO Developer Hub.
Open the Say, Pi add-on → Upload New Version → dist/saypi.firefox.xpi.
Let addons-linter validate (warnings are OK and won't block; errors block).
Paste the "What's new" text into Release Notes for this Version.
Paste the build steps + eval note into Notes for Reviewers.
gotchas: Source archive is MANDATORY for our minified build — the reviewer rebuilds from it and diffs against the shipped package; it must match. Include build instructions.

Reproducibility caveat: .env.production values are baked into the build, so a reviewer cannot reproduce byte-for-byte without them — confirm how AMO source review is handled if the reviewer queries it.
The onnxruntime eval() warning is a non-blocking warning (not an error).

api.auth: AMO JWT issuer + secret from addons.mozilla.org/developers/addon/api/key (env WEB_EXT_API_KEY / WEB_EXT_API_SECRET)
api.note: Supports --upload-source-code=source-code.zip and release notes via --amo-metadata. gecko id gecko@saypi.ai maps the upload to the say-pi listing. See references/firefox-amo.md.
